questions.question: What is a stoma?
options: A tube passed into a vein to give fluids and medicines
A dressing placed over a wound to keep it clean and dry
A machine that measures a person's blood pressure
An opening made by surgery on the tummy that lets bowel motions or urine leave the body into a pouch

questions.question: Why is a pouch worn over a stoma?
options: Because it replaces the need for hand hygiene
Because it stops the stoma from feeling pain
Because it is needed to give the stoma its pink colour
Because the person can no longer control when waste comes out, so it collects the waste

questions.question: Which type of stoma is formed from the large bowel and usually passes fairly formed, solid stools?
options: An ileostomy
A tracheostomy
A colostomy
A urostomy

questions.question: Which type of stoma is formed from the end of the small bowel, the ileum, and passes looser, more watery output?
options: A urostomy
A colostomy
An ileostomy
A gastrostomy

questions.question: What does a urostomy drain?
options: Urine, carried out using a short piece of bowel
Air from the lungs
Bile from the liver
Formed, solid stools

questions.question: In the digestive system, where is most of the water absorbed to form waste into stools?
options: In the bladder
In the stomach only
In the large bowel, or colon
In the kidneys

questions.question: Which of these is given in the course as a common reason a person may have a stoma?
options: A minor grazed knee
Having a common cold
Bowel or bladder cancer, or inflammatory bowel disease such as Crohn's disease
Wearing glasses for short sight

questions.question: What is the difference between a temporary and a permanent stoma?
options: A temporary stoma drains urine and a permanent one drains bowel motions
A temporary stoma gives part of the bowel time to rest and heal before it is later reversed, while a permanent one is not reversed
There is no difference; the words mean the same thing
A temporary stoma is smaller and a permanent one is always larger

questions.question: The pouch and the sticky part that fixes it to the skin are together called the what?
options: The filter
The appliance
The template
The night bag

questions.question: In a two-piece appliance, how are the parts arranged?
options: There is only a baseplate and no pouch
Two separate pouches are worn at the same time
The baseplate stays stuck to the skin while the pouch clips or sticks onto it
The pouch and baseplate are joined as a single unit changed together

questions.question: Which pouch is sealed and taken off and thrown away when full, and is often used with a colostomy?
options: A urostomy pouch with a tap
A drainable pouch
A night bag
A closed pouch

questions.question: A urostomy pouch typically has which feature at the bottom to drain urine?
options: A built-in sponge
A clip seal that is thrown away each time
A tap or valve that can connect to a larger night bag
No opening at all

questions.question: As a general guide, when should a drainable or urostomy pouch be emptied?
options: Exactly once a week regardless of contents
Never; it is only ever replaced, not emptied
When it is around one-third to one-half full
Only once it is bulging and completely full

questions.question: Why is a pouch emptied before it becomes too full and heavy?
options: Because a full pouch stops the filter deodorising gas
Because a heavy pouch drags on the baseplate and can break the seal and leak
Because a full pouch changes the stoma's colour
Because it is the only time hand hygiene is needed

questions.question: When changing an appliance, what should you use to clean the stoma and surrounding skin?
options: Perfumed soap and moisturiser
Antiseptic solution and oily wipes
Plain water, drying the skin well afterwards
Alcohol gel rubbed directly onto the stoma

questions.question: Which change in output should be reported because it can be an early sign that something is wrong?
options: The person eating a balanced meal
The pouch filter letting gas escape slowly
Output that looks and smells exactly as usual
Output that is much greater or much less than normal, very watery, or that contains blood

questions.question: How should a used stoma pouch be disposed of?
options: Buried in the garden
Left on the side to reuse later
Placed in a disposal bag and put into the general household or clinical waste as directed
Flushed down the toilet

questions.question: How does the built-in filter on most modern pouches help?
options: It changes the colour of the stoma
It measures the amount of urine passed
It replaces the need for a baseplate
It lets gas escape slowly while deodorising it, so the pouch does not balloon

questions.question: Before and after any contact with a person's stoma or pouch, what must you do?
options: Wear gloves for the whole shift without changing them
Only put on gloves; handwashing is not needed if gloves are worn
Decontaminate your hands and wear clean disposable gloves and an apron
Rely on the pouch filter to keep things clean

questions.question: Which statement about gloves and hand hygiene is correct?
options: Gloves are a full substitute for washing your hands
Gloves are never a substitute for hand hygiene
Hand hygiene is only needed if the person has an infection
You only need to clean your hands before, not after, the task

questions.question: Which of these best describes a healthy stoma?
options: Pink or red, moist and shiny, with no pain when cleaned and perhaps a little mucus
Dry, cracked and flaking on the surface
Very painful to touch during cleaning
Pale, grey or dusky in colour and cold to touch

questions.question: Why does cleaning a stoma not hurt the person?
options: Because the pouch numbs the area
Because pain relief is always given first
Because a stoma has no nerve endings
Because the stoma is covered by thick skin

questions.question: What is the skin around the stoma called, and how should it look when healthy?
options: The flange; it should be red and weepy
The peristomal skin; it should look the same as the rest of the tummy, intact and not sore
The template; it should be cracked and dry
The baseplate; it should be moist and shiny

questions.question: What is the most common cause of sore peristomal skin?
options: The stoma having no nerve endings
Drinking too much water
Leakage, when output creeps under the baseplate and sits against the skin
Cleaning the stoma with plain water

questions.question: If you notice the skin becoming red, sore, itchy or broken, or the appliance not staying stuck, what should you do?
options: Rub the area firmly to improve the blood supply
Report it to the stoma nurse, who can check the fit and treat the skin properly
Treat it yourself with creams and extra tape
Ignore it, as sore skin always settles on its own

questions.question: Which eating habit is described as helping prevent a blockage, especially for people with an ileostomy?
options: Chewing food well and not rushing meals
Avoiding all fluids with meals
Eating as fast as possible
Only eating nuts and sweetcorn

questions.question: Why is good hydration especially important for people with an ileostomy?
options: Because it changes the colour of the stoma to pink
Because it removes the need to wear a pouch
Because it stops the stoma producing any mucus
Because the watery output passes before the large bowel can reabsorb water and salts, risking dehydration

questions.question: A person's stoma has turned from its normal pink-red colour to a dusky, dark purple. What should you do?
options: Assume it is normal and note it for the next care review
Treat it as urgent and get help straight away, as a colour change can mean the blood supply is affected
Rub the stoma to bring the colour back
Put extra tape over the pouch and carry on

questions.question: What is a prolapse of the stoma?
options: When the stoma turns pale and cold
When the stoma becomes longer and sticks out much more than usual
When the peristomal skin becomes weepy
When the stoma sinks below the level of the skin

questions.question: Which set of signs might suggest a stoma blockage that needs reporting?
options: The person drinking plenty of fluids through the day
Little or no output, cramping tummy pain, a swollen tummy and feeling sick or vomiting
A pink, moist stoma and intact, healthy skin around it
A steady, normal amount of output and the person feeling completely well

questions.question: If you suspect a blockage in a person with an ileostomy, what does the course advise?
options: Wait several days before mentioning it
Push the stoma back in yourself
Encourage them to eat a large solid meal
Stop the person eating solid food, encourage sips of fluid if they can manage them, and report it promptly

questions.question: Which of these is a sign of dehydration to watch for and report?
options: Bright, plentiful pale urine and no thirst
A dry mouth, strong thirst, dark and reduced urine, cramps and dizziness
A well-fitting, leak-free appliance
A pink, moist stoma

questions.question: How can living with a stoma affect a person emotionally?
options: It can affect body image and confidence, and people may feel anxious, low or worried about leaks and odour
It changes the person's blood pressure permanently
It only ever makes people feel more confident
It has no emotional impact at all

questions.question: What is the best way to support a person emotionally with their stoma?
options: Be calm, patient and non-judgemental, listen to their worries, protect their dignity and encourage independence
Take over all their care so they do nothing themselves
Rush the care so it is over quickly and avoid talking about it
Tell them they must never leave the house

questions.question: This course on stoma care provides which of the following?
options: Permission to diagnose and treat stoma complications
A licence to change the person's prescribed products yourself
Full authorisation to carry out any stoma care immediately
Awareness only; it does not replace task-specific training, assessed competence, the care plan or the stoma nurse's assessment

questions.question: A drainable stoma pouch should usually be emptied before it becomes too full and heavy, at around one-third to one-half full.
options: True
False

questions.question: A used stoma pouch should be flushed down the toilet to dispose of it.
options: True
False

questions.question: You should always dry the skin thoroughly before fitting a new stoma appliance.
options: True
False

questions.question: A stoma has no nerve endings, so cleaning it does not hurt the person.
options: True
False

questions.question: A pale, grey, purple or black stoma is normal and does not need to be reported.
options: True
False

questions.question: People with an ileostomy or urostomy are at greater risk of dehydration and should be encouraged to drink well unless told otherwise for another medical reason.
options: True
False

questions.question: Wearing gloves means you do not need to wash your hands before and after stoma care.
options: True
False

questions.question: A small amount of bleeding from the surface of the stoma when it is cleaned is normal.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of the following are the three main types of stoma described in the course? (Select all that apply)
options: Urostomy
Colostomy
Ileostomy
Gastrostomy
Tracheostomy

questions.question: Which of the following help protect a person's dignity and privacy during stoma care? (Select all that apply)
options: Working in a private space with doors and curtains closed
Gaining the person's consent and explaining each step
Keeping the person covered as much as possible
Discussing their stoma loudly in front of visitors
Being matter-of-fact and not rushing

questions.question: Which of the following help keep peristomal skin healthy? (Select all that apply)
options: Changing the appliance at the right intervals so it does not leak
Cleaning with plain water and drying the skin thoroughly
Applying oily wipes and moisturisers around the stoma
Making sure the baseplate opening matches the size and shape of the stoma
Leaving a leaking appliance in place and re-taping it

questions.question: Which of the following are warning signs of a possible stoma blockage? (Select all that apply)
options: Cramping tummy pain and a swollen tummy
Little or no output from the stoma
A swollen stoma
A pink, moist stoma with healthy skin
Feeling sick or vomiting

questions.question: Which changes to the stoma or its output should be reported to the stoma nurse? (Select all that apply)
options: A pink, moist stoma passing its normal output
The stoma turning pale, grey or dark
Heavy bleeding or bleeding that does not stop
A retraction, where the stoma sinks below the skin
A prolapse, where the stoma sticks out much more than usual

questions.question: Which of the following are signs of dehydration to watch for in a person with a stoma? (Select all that apply)
options: Bright, plentiful pale urine
Dark and reduced urine
Confusion, if it becomes severe
Muscle cramps, tiredness and dizziness
A very dry mouth and strong thirst

questions.question: Which of the following are correct when carrying out delegated stoma care? (Select all that apply)
options: Support the person's own independence wherever they can manage
Only carry it out if trained, assessed as competent and it is in the care plan
Report anything abnormal to the stoma nurse or registered nurse
Switch to different products whenever you think it best
Clean your hands and put on gloves and an apron before starting

questions.question: A stoma made from the large bowel is called a ___.
A stoma made from the end of the small bowel is called an ___.
A stoma that drains urine rather than bowel motions is called a ___.
The skin around the stoma is called the ___ skin.
A used stoma pouch is placed in a disposal bag and put into the general waste; it is never flushed down the ___.
